# Git basics

git init        # create a new repository in the current directory
git add <file>  # stage changes for the next commit
git commit -m   # record staged changes as a new commit with a message
git branch      # list or create branches
git merge       # combine another branch's history into the current branch
git clone <url> # copy a remote repository, including its full history

# A commit is identified by a SHA-1 hash. Branches are movable pointers to
# commits. The staging area (index) holds changes before they are committed.
